Description: Used for distributing general notices or disclosures to participants (e.g., legal updates, plan notices).
Tracks when a participant has requested a callback after leaving a voicemail.
Marks participant registrations or follow-ups related to educational webinars or learning sessions.
Used when a participant is enrolling or updating elections in their 401(k) plan (e.g., setting contributions, investment choices).
Tracks participant submissions for taking out a loan from their 401(k) plan.
Used for questions or issues regarding participants' access to or use of their dashboard.

A distribution made after a participant leaves their employer. Once they’ve separated service, they can take their vested account balance (lump sum or installments) per plan rules and tax regulations.
Flags requests from participants seeking a distribution due to financial hardship, as permitted under IRS rules (e.g., medical expenses, eviction prevention).
Marks issues where participant contributions are affected by errors or delays in payroll processing.
Tags when a participant has completed a RightSignature form needed for processing (e.g., loan or withdrawal forms).
Flags support requests related to multi-factor authentication setup or troubleshooting for participant accounts.
Used when participants inquire about the tax implications of distributions, loans, or contributions.
Applied when participants initiate or inquire about rolling assets from another retirement plan into their 401(k).
A withdrawal a participant takes while still employed. Plans may allow access to funds—often for participants age 59½+ without terminating employment.

A court-ordered payment under a Qualified Domestic Relations Order. It directs the participant's plan to pay all or part of their benefits to an alternate payee (often a former spouse) as part of divorce or legal separation.

A payout to a participant's named beneficiaries when they die. Beneficiaries may choose a lump sum, installments, or rollover into an inherited IRA, following plan terms and IRS required-minimum-distribution rules.

The smallest yearly withdrawal the IRS requires from retirement accounts once they reach age 73 (or later if laws change). It ensures tax-deferred savings get distributed and avoids a 50% penalty on unmet amounts.
Other recordkeepers’ participant inquiries or processes related to Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs).
Applied when participants submit or inquire about Qualified Domestic Relations Orders (QDROs) or Domestic Relations Orders (DROs) for splitting retirement assets due to divorce or legal separation.
Flags inquiries or requests related to cryptocurrency investment options within the participant’s 401(k) plan.
Used when a participant schedules or attends a one-on-one advisory session for financial guidance or retirement planning.
Marks inquiries about investment options, including Self-Directed Brokerage Accounts (SDBA) or changes to fund selections.
Applied when processing refunds related to Eligible Automatic Contribution Arrangements (EACA), such as refunds of excess contributions.
Used when participants submit, update, or inquire about their beneficiary designations.
Flags participant questions or updates about contribution rates, such as percentage or dollar amount elections.
Applied when refunding participant contributions that exceed IRS limits or plan contribution caps.
Flags tickets that require communication or support to be provided in Spanish.
